  "purpose": "This code defines a module that loads a CSS template generated by an external 'template-fn' library and dynamically injects it into the document's head as a style element.",
  "sources": "The code reads the external module 'template-fn' via require, define, or global variable, and also reads document.head and document.createElement.",
  "sinks": "The code creates and appends a <style> element with innerHTML set to the result of the 'template-fn' function output, injecting CSS into the DOM.",
  "flows": "Input: 'template-fn' module → output string 'n' → create style element with innerHTML = 'n' → append to document.head.",
  "anomalies": "The code uses an internal cache 'r' to prevent multiple injections of the same CSS. No hardcoded secrets or unusual code patterns. No obfuscated or minified suspicious code. The dynamic injection pattern is typical for CSS-in-JS implementations.",
  "analysis": "The code is a standard module pattern supporting CommonJS, AMD, or global variable. It imports a 'template-fn' module, applies it with arguments, caches the result to avoid duplicate style injections, and injects the generated CSS into the document. The injection is straightforward, with no signs of data exfiltration, malicious payloads, or hidden behaviors. It does not perform any network requests, file operations, or suspicious actions. Overall, it appears to be a benign CSS injection utility.",
  "conclusion": "This code is a typical dynamic CSS injection module that loads CSS templates and injects them into the DOM. No malicious or suspicious behaviors are evident. It is a benign utility for styling purposes.",
